commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=7128c2d4c37c4f8f417f28ff193a1cf000525c8c)
commands.review_summary:
The branch adds `--agent` handling to `infer/capability model auth logout`, updates focused CLI tests, and adds a changelog entry for the non-default agent auth-store fix.

Reproducibility: yes. The linked issue gives a concrete env-scoped logout path, and current source shows logout always resolves the default agent directory instead of the env override.
commands.review_followup: Needs PR revision and contributor proof: the branch adds explicit `--agent` support but leaves the linked env-scoped logout workflow unhandled.

target.title: fix(cli): honor agent for model auth logout
target.body:
Summary
- Add the missing `--agent` option to `capability model auth logout`.
- Route logout through the selected agent directory while preserving the configured default-agent behavior.
- Add regression coverage for default and non-default agent auth stores.

Verification
Behavior addressed: `openclaw capability model auth logout --provider <id> --agent <id>` now removes profiles from the requested agent store instead of always using the default agent store.
Real environment tested: local macOS source checkout.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cli/capability-cli.test.ts`; `git diff --check`; `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local`
Evidence after fix: regression test asserts both load and write-lock paths receive `/tmp/agent-poe` when `--agent poe` is passed.
Observed result after fix: focused test passed, diff check passed, autoreview reported no accepted/actionable findings.
What was not tested: live auth logout against a real non-default agent profile store.

Fixes #85092
target.author: steipete

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
